column65: Despite certain people every now and then doomposting about the Love Live!'s demise, I think it's doing quite well. The only reason they're saying that is because they don't have access to 95% of the content they push out (collabs, popup shops, lives, the only existing game, likely unable to read Japanese as well). Staff IS trying their best to make their things accessible worldwide (multi-language subs/availability), which thankfully isn't region-locked most of the time. I guess some people just need to be spoonfed directly on Love Live! content if it's not available to them. With the current direction of Bluebird, I'll say it can be better. It's nice to hear about the idols' lives through tweets, but there could be at least a picture or two here and there, maybe idols could interact with each other as well? Other than that, I love this new concept and how everything except for the tweets are going. Love Live! is, and will be fine, it's not one of the most high grossing series in Japan for nothing.
column66: Outside of LL!Days interactions I'd love to see Bluebird girls interact more on Twitter. It's kinda nice seeing new cards drop bi-weekly and seeing everyone get excited, it's the one thing that brought everyone together alongside a new anime episode. And speaking of which, a new anime (with hopefully better writing direction) is well needed. ok listen as long as it isn't genjitsu season 2 i am SO fucking fine with anything.  ooh ooh also they should put tsukasa in ooooone more hasu episode. just for me. pretty please. dead giveaway hi gang

column65: I'm not really good on expressing it, but, I do think it's mostly fine. Apart from the SIF fiasco. It offers a pretty good amount of content, the thing is that it might not be from the group you're most connected with, but with this said I think Love Live is one of the franchises that at least tries to balance this the most, ironically. And might not be the type of content you're most connected with, but you can't blame the franchise for that. Unfortunately the SIF fiasco left other groups in the dark with app/game, apart from Hasunosora, but being only this one group and not overseas distributed is limiting. However Link Like is a very ambitious project on it's own, and I'm always impressed they could deliver and how compromised they are with the experience, so it's the project/group I respect the most nowadays, along with them having the most consistent quality. Going to another topic, I also think that, anime-wise, they are fine? Like, there has no stop from producing anime, it's just that I think they need the right time and sort of let each group do their thing first then go to the next. But I must say I can maybe criticize on their pacing or writing quality. But I'll leave it at that.

column65: I appreciate the experimentation we are getting from staff but I wish that they we would receive more content overall. It's unfair to say that we aren't getting ANY content, but since the focus is mostly on live performances now more than ever, it is a bit exhausting for someone who enjoys all aspects of the series. Hasu is doing great and I'm okay with their handling on it, though I'm worried to see how things will be once this new year rolls around. Bluebird is interesting and is still in it's infancy, so I am also okay with the current pace of things on that end too. However when it comes to both Niji and especially Liella currently it feels as if staff is a bit lost with where to take them. Niji is at least getting some fun stuff with the monthly songs (even if I am not too big of a fan of them so far), but I worry that once the movies finish up they will be in the same limbo as Liella currently in where the only content we get of them is live performances. I love seiyuu content but my god, it feels like staff doesn't know what to do with these two groups without a mobage providing a constant stream of content for the characters themselves.
column66: As probably everyone else will say, a cross group mobage is desperately needed. Other than that, more content focused on the characters themselves would be nice. It doesn't have to be a continuation of their stories, but stuff like MVs and miniseries'/sidestories for Liella and Niji would go a long way.

column65: While Love Live is thriving in Japan, I do worry about its decline in popularity in the West. For many people the games were an easily accessible way to get into the franchise and Hasunosora being region locked may prevent newer fans from getting into it. I’ve also been noticing a trend of Love Live falling out of the spotlight at conventions (specifically U.S. conventions). Conventions used to be full of Love Live merchandise or cosplay groups, but those numbers have declined over the years. It saddens me and I really wish I could do something to make Love Live THE idol franchise again. Love Live still puts out plenty of content but for non-Japanese speaking fans it may dissuade them from getting into the series. A completely new fan isn’t always going to know where to look to find information out.
column66: I would love to have a globally marketed game again. The Yohane games are excellent, sure, but I just really miss the core elements of SIF. When SIFAS simplified the rhythm elements I was a little disappointed but I’d take still having SIFAS over having nothing (not counting the arcade games because no arcade near me has them.) I also wish Love Live would put more effort into marketing the franchise to newer fans. Seeing umamusume getting everything right with their advertising makes me mourn what we could be having…

column65: I feel quite content following the current official content offered such as Link! Like! Love Live! and Ikizurai-bu posts (thankful for enough basic Japanese and tech knowledge to know how to install/work around region-locked content and understanding the stories), however,  it seems quite discouraging to interact with the majority of fellow English-speaking fans who complain that Love Live! is currently lacking content when there are an abundance, just need effort to get into (which I get their frustration, being a fan since Aqours started at 2016 myself official content was easier to find anywhere and SIF was more accessible for everyone). I personally am content, just that seeing other anidol fandoms thrive such as Project Sekai and the recent global hype of Uma Musume makes me understand the frustration of global fans, though I feel like they over-exaggerate things since there are worse fandoms with lack of accessible content for overseas fans out there (Idolm@ster, Pretty Series to name a few and I'm also a part of those fandoms but most of us are chiller about the lack of accessible English content).
column66: While I understand it's hard for Love Live! to get back to their peak era of popularity (around Mu's and Aqours era I believe), I still think that it's possible with the latest AiScReam boom almost paralleling that of Nico Nico Nii and Snow Halation memes back then. I'm content with whatever the devs have in mind, though anime media would personally be enjoyable for me since I was first introduced to Love Live! from Love Live! Sunshine!! and would love to see peak writing (especially Link! Like! Love Live!'s and excited to see what Love Live! Bluebird has to offer) adapted to animation

The Bluebird was even more disappointing in the sense that the series seems fragmented being posted primarily online, not to mention the art style with flat colors and minimalistic design really couldn’t get me interested in any of them. Personally I think they need to stop cheaping out releasing contents only in game or social media, and stop rotating groups/generations so fast as it is difficult to get a loyal fan base to support these new groups.
column66: (1) More well developed groups like how Mus, Aqours, and Nijigaku were done. Honestly I couldn’t care less if they were true to the “school idol” years, I personally rather keep seeing contents from my favorite group than having new groups being adding every year and then graduating the next. (2) More diverse music. Nijigaku has been my favorite because of this very reason. Don’t get me wrong, I like LL having its signature genki/classic happy idol songs, but branching out to include more different genres really add more flavor to the whole discography.

column65: I firmly believe it would do them a huge service to give more attention to their western audience. While I don’t think it should be an expectation that everything is perfectly translated and catered to a western audience that’s less present in this moment, the way they’ve handled their games and lack of subtitling or giving any notice at all to other regions has had a very big impact on Love Live’s reach and performance. Love Live has shown that it can still grasp people’s attention (like AiScream) but it feels like they’ve tried to pivot to new things (like Hasunosora and Bluebird) but fail to realize why their old content did so well and how it gained such a big audience. I don’t expect every group to last forever and get the same treatment, but the need to innovate makes them lose their original audience and appeal that drew everyone in in the first place.
column66: I’d love to see more blending of different groups performing and letting older songs from different love live franchises be covered! I liked the twitter storytelling format originally and admire love live for looking to new and creative ways to capture people’s attention and tell a story, but I do really think they could benefit from a new/revamped mobile game that allows for more than just a japanese audience.

Regarding the rest of the groups, I think they're on a good position (minus muse who's getting nostalgia milked). Aqours having its finale, Niji with its monthly songs, upcoming flower based live and the second movie, and idk about liella cuz I don't follow them as much but it seems that everything is ok for them. Either way the lack of a game or games to tie everything together or have a way to interact with Love Live daily is what is killing us.

column65: I was a big fan of Aqours and Nijigasaki but personally really disliked liella to the point of never watching season 3 and not at all keeping up with Hasu, Bluebird, the musical and frankly anything that wasn't the occasional Aqours single before they disbanded and the Nijigasaki movie. But a part of me thinks that I personally have perhaps outgrown love live and intirests have shifted to other things. Wether that's due to quality or my personal preferences changing is your call but Liella left me with a bad taste meanwhile I've loved the Nijigasaki ova and the movie I'm always looking foward to. It'll always have a special place in my heart with many fun memories and friends made through the community...but dear God the twitter community could also make experiencing Love Live so much worse... Not the main reason I quit but God it was a minefield somedays and frankly I think I'm happy where I'm at with love live poking in and out whenever Nijigasaki does something like fun or the wierd bluebird stuff. From my view it feels like ALOT or constant content potentially to much but I'm happy only experiencing Nijigasaki and I don't think anybody is forcing anyone to experience ALL of love live. I had my fun with love live and for my friends who stick with it I hope there happy but personally I've mostly moved on and am content as a mainly Nijigasaki fan ♡
column66: More High quality Nijigasaki content and as a shitpost a love live fighting game. As mentioned in the previous question I'm mostly done with love live and frankly anything I'd want would either be a joke or something I don't think a majority of the community would want. I hope whoever sticks with love live has a good time but as long as Nijigasaki stays consistently great I'm sadisfied and is all I could ask for. Tho again love live fighting game would go crazy assuming it's not another god damn mobile gacha game because SIF 2 and late all stars left me with such a bad taste... my real request for the future is no more gacha games 🙏

column65: I personally think there’s a lot going on. Sometimes it’s too much for me to keep up with. They’re definitely a bit of a void left by Aqours slowing down their activity, as well as the Superstar anime likely being over, though. If Hasu and Link Like wasn’t around, I’d definitely feel it more. I’m the kind of fan that wants to keep up with all the groups in all media forms, so I think I notice the lack of anime/games less than other fans, though. I also have a lot of feelings about staff cutting out 102nd out of almost all content except the manga. I feel like they could still do merch for them and stuff. I understand they aren’t going to include the seiyuus in events and stuff, and I actually like that a lot (also gives the opportunity to see them do other stuff, as well as being a smaller commitment for seiyuu so there is less likelihood for the group to be disbanded or put on hiatus due to seiyuu wanting to do work outside of LL. Not to mention it’s really built into Hasu’s themes and story), but I feel like pretending they don’t exist in promo material, particularly for the anniversary, is sticking to the bit a little too much. I hope Ui-sama, Konachi and Kocchan are at least invited to LL fes, or their characters are acknowledged in some way.
column66: I would love to see more multi group events like the Asia tour, as well as more multi-group songs. I would also like more shuffle events, like Nijigaku’s shuffle festival. A new rhythm game would be nice too!

column65: I do realize it's been getting less popular / attention overseas, which kind of saddens me. I don't have problems with region-locking as I speak Japanese but it's hard to translate all the content that comes up (hasunosora's hours longs story, although there is a translation in progress, ikizulive daily tweets, etc...). Regardless, I don't think the franchise is doing as bad as some people claim (but pleasee can we get a game with all the characters). Other than that I do heavily dislike, as a person who uploads covers, the fact that ikizulive is making a "list of songs you're allowed to cover" that gets updated unconstantly and leaves out songs randomly (himitsu michi, as an exmaple, has been out since july and has an MV and all, yet iy's not in the list, therefore you can't cover it). The b-sides from the solo songs also aren't there, and the list was updated yesterday (September 27th) for the first time in almost 2 months + the way it's being handled is not organized at all and it looks like 4 people working on a group project where everyone does their part separately and then put it together at the end.
column66: Actual consistency on the way Ikizulive content is uploaded and more collabs between groups, as well as the appearence of the graduated members of Hasunosora since, even if I do like the concept of cycling members (in the end that's how it'd work realistically), I don't like that they're left out completely of stuff like merch or birthday café events.

column65: I think that in terms of content, music, and live shows, it more than meets the mark. The music more than delivers and is released at a good pace, and the different groups and musical styles mean that if you don't like one group, you can like another, so that seems perfect to me. My main complaint would be how they handled Hasu and perhaps Bluebird, which is where I feel most disconnected. With Hasu and the graduation in real time, I think they've killed off three characters who were very necessary and beloved to the plot, and who have been relegated to oblivion, breaking many of their concepts. For Bluebird, my complaint is mostly about the content, which I think is difficult to follow the story or give context to the characters without an animated series/game or something where I see them interact.
column66: As for me, I think I'd like to see more collaborative lives like Koshien or joint shows with Idolmaster and other groups. I'd like to see a joint show with Bang Dream or Uma. Although the former isn't idol-themed, they can combine different musical styles with many of the groups in Love Live. On a separate note, I'd like to see some tours in Spain or Europe with one of the Love Live groups, like with Liella, who wanted to make themselves known worldwide and have their music transcend borders. I hope they give the girls the opportunity to see the support they have outside of Japan and Asian countries.

column65: While I like that the series is now in a precure-like state of generations, I do wish we got more inter-unit content… I like the magazine content because of that. I also think something important is that, while people who say love live doesn’t get anything anymore are… objectively wrong, it’s notable that most content we get for love live currently mainly almost solely appeals to pre-existing fans. Concerts, new music, and the card game are all really incredible, but none of them will actually draw in a new crowd, y’know? It worries me because it could easily lead to a bad state. When the anime is coming out, or there’s a game, there’s something to almost careen people into. When AiScream blew up, as an example, it never extended because there was nothing to extend TO. Sure, there were three separate anime to watch for one member each, but when it’s both that much to catch up on and has nothing to actually do with what hooked you, you’re not going to stick around. It’s a shame, too. Love live is capitalizing on the popularity of it at concerts, but how many times with people with AiScream and little else, if anything at all, stick around?
column66: Literally anything ongoing and easily accessible and Modernized would be ideal. An ongoing anime season is the most likely result, so I will not get my hopes up. However, a game in the more recent style of rhythm mobage would be ideal. I’d prefer for it to be more like SIFAS where there’s a sense of interconnectivity, myself. Ideally, it would include everyone… I wouldn’t mind a deresute situation where the N Girls are included and unvoiced. Characters like the A-RISE members could even have a situation more like the non-cryptonloids in project diva, where they got to appear but had no voice lines or anything. I guess both these things are actually fairly old-school concepts, though LOL.
column67: Yes, but there could be more

column65: for me, the peak was 2019 to 2023 (a lot of people would say the SIF era was the peak, which is fair cause what i miss the most about old love live was playing the rhythm minigame in SIF) i think the music was the best it had ever been, the content we were getting from sifas was amazing, and it was the peak of my involvement in the fandom… however i do love the experimentation that hasu and bluebird are having in this modern era. now that i think about it more, you can really separate each era really well by which games were active haha. anyway, i think μ’s/aqours/nijigasaki are really taking a backseat to let the newer groups shine, which is absolutely natural given the passage of time but i do really love and miss μ’s/aqours/nijigasaki. i tried to connect with liella but i found it very difficult (no hate to them at all), hasunosora’s content output is so insanely rapid that i can’t really keep up at all but i think they’re a very cool and unique group and EDEL NOTE IS THE BEST SUBUNIT TO COME OUT OF LOVE LIVE OH MY GOODNESS. ABSOLUTE PEAK. i have a few issues with the way bluebird is operating (NEVER EVER LET THE FANS VOTE PLEASE IT ALMOST ALWAYS ENDS IN A WAY I DISLIKE!!!!!!!!!) BUT i do think the slower pace compared to hasu makes it easier to keep up with even if it’s been losing a bit of momentum. i really enjoy the bluebird characters and the concept REALLY intrigues me (like is this an alternate universe????) and i am very interested in what the project will do. the characters seem quite one note at the moment (midori comes to mind even though she’s my absolute favourite my baby and my daughter) but i think that’s just due to the project being so early in its lifespan. anyway i’m not going to proof read this ramble so i’m sorry if things don’t make sense but TLDR: newer groups are cool and unique, but i’m upset at how the older groups have to take a backseat due to the passage of time, and i miss 2019-2023 love live era the most
column66: people really want a new game, which so do i, but the older seiyuus (namely the μ’s seiyuus) probably wouldn’t be able to voice in it the same way they did in sif/sifas/sif2. what i REALLY want is an offline mobile game where you can freeplay sif beatmaps and new beatmaps are added for new songs, but that’s probably not something that would never happen sadly. i want a lot more μ’s content but i don’t think that will happen either. i want a song with all the leaders in it, that would be awesome. ooooo or a HUGE franchise-wide cover of sunny day song. i’d die for that. also i want more edginess in love live, maybe not in an ave monica but in a mygo way. give us canon couples. and ooh i want more nijigasaki subunit songs ITS BEEN 3 YEARS. omg also what if we get a bluebird anime and we had cameos from μ’s and aqours i think i’d sob… okay that’s enough rambling
column67: No, but there is enough to keep me around

column65: It feels like we're being strangled. I genuinely have no idea what LL Staff is doing. Ikizulive is an okay concept, but they're no replacement for Aqours. Hasunosora is not only not available to English speakers, but is fated to die in less than a year with the graduation of its main character. With the conclusion of Liella's anime, and with no movie in sight, the 2D side of Liella is effectively gone, which hurts for me especially as a Liella fan. Liella is entering a phase similar to Aqours, where new releases are few and far between, and the fans can only wait for their finale to come. Muse is gone, Aqours is gone, Nijigasaki was made for a game that no longer exists, Hasunosora will likely be gone within a year, and Ikizulive is slow with a weird format. Only Liella remains, but it feels like staff doesn't care as much about them.
column66: The answer on everyone's lips is that we need a new mobile game, preferably a rhythm game. Love Live was able to command such a wide, international audience because of the success of School Idol Festival, and now that it's gone it feels like we have nothing.

The song descriptions, the gacha pull animations for each group, little animated stories for each group as well. column65: It's in a bad state, to be frank. The attempts at innovation are not ahead of its time at all - the most recent attempts to do twitter idols without the actresses behind the idols being able to organically interact with each other already shows how out of touch the writers/producers are with the current climate of fans who are into idol-like genres. Uma Musume was having their VAs Vtube and livestream as their characters back in 2021. Things are only getting more "outdated" with the 2010s-eqsue A-1 Pictures artstyle of the latest group. There's cleaely enough engagement from JP fans going to concerts and buying the magazine, etc. to keep the franchise afloat but interest from idol and anime fans has been hemorrhaging ever since SIF first became a viral hit back in the 2010s.
column66: A raising sim game with actual graphics comparable to what's needed to go viral today (so fully rigged and high quality models like Uma Musume or Genshin Impact at the least). Something lole DereSute would already be too outdated at this point. The game having rhythm game elements is also a no brainers. At this point I don't think the game needs to encompass every group to hit big, it just needs to be good enough at a general gameplay level and have solid writing for the characters it does cover. Most idol franchises rely on a mobile game/console game as their backbone so that's the only thing at this point I could see seriously bringing back major interest to the franchise on both a domestic and global level.

column65: Personally, it feels like the connection to the global community has been in a very dire state. there's no anime or game to tie the entire community together in one place, but rather it's split into several fragments because of the seperate media that are being used to show the new groups. I wouldn't say I haven't fallen out of Love Live yet, I still am a massive fan of μ's, Aqours and Nijigasaki, but ever since the second season of Liella it feels very... loose. I've never been able to interact with Hasunosora and BlueBird because... well I don't speak or read Japanese so there's no centralised way to experience these stories, characters and songs in English. Which means that emotionally, the series stopped at Liella season 2.
column66: a new centralized platform like a (mobile) game or normal anime series etc with GOOD and FAR REACHING global connection to experience all the Hasunosora and BlueBird stories, in addition to future entries.

column65: I think the state of Love Live is fine. I’m a long time follower of many anisong projects. I’ve been following Love Live since the muse days but it’s also not the only series I’m into. I know fans love to joke about how SIF2 was handled and yeah, it was a hot mess. I’ve also seen a lot of other mobile games have similar fates though. In the 2010s there was a lot that would come out and struggle to last year. While they were also meant to be “multi media projects” like Love Live is, since the game does everything else relating to the franchise went on a complete haunt. Since I’ve seen it so many times I can say with absolute positivity that I know that that isn’t the case with Love Live. It’s doing fine! So much stuff is coming out still if you look at the release schedule. I understand as I also was someone who was most into Love Live when I was actively playing SIF, but I wish fans wouldn’t see their WANT for a new game as a lack of the franchise doing anything. So much is coming out, the seiyuu and staff still put a lot of work into it. Please support them.
column66: I’m enjoying seeing how experimental Love Live has been lately! I hope they keep it up even if every choice hasn’t been my favorite. column65: I've been a fan of Love Live for 8 years now, and I feel like I've literally seen the rise and fall of the franchise when it comes to global fanbase. They've been making terrible decision after terrible decision that's making this franchise unrecognizable - changing the iconic artstyle, newer groups not getting an anime adaptation, shutting down SIF/SIFAS/SIF 2, and making any form of new content for Hasunosora and LL Bluebird so undigestable and you'd have to be a hardcore Love Live fan to even want to get into those two groups. I think it's great that Love Live still has a passionate fanbase dedicated to making translations, but it's stupid we even have to rely on something like that at all just to be a FAN these days. Liella, imo, was the last group in the series that felt like Love Live at all. I understand they want to experiment with the series to expand and not just recycle the same formula over and over, but I feel like they can do that and still honor Love Live's identity as a franchise, Nijigasaki in SIFAS is proof that's possible. I'm still a Love Live fan, but I do not pay attention to any of the new content outside of what is surface level information. Call me when they release SIF 3 with all the groups in it
column66: I want a live service gacha game for Love Live that features all of the characters in it, and I want the card rotations to be like they were in SIFAS where each group got a card for one girl at a time I don't care if it's difficult for them, and I want it to be like SIFAS but with actual good gameplay. I want GNY season 2 where we actually get to see Riko use her fire powers, I want to see Rikako in more lives, I want Guilty Kiss to release more music, and I want Aqours to get more content every so often that isn't just a Hasunosora cover or a magazine scan

column65: Bushiroad's mismanagement of the mobile games is borderline criminal. I think the demise of SIFAS and the debacle with SIF2 did permanent damage to the reputation of the franchise and caused a lot of people to move on. Hasunosora being locked behind a JP-only mobile app has killed any interest in them among general international audiences, even though (in my opinion) their songs are among the best in the entire franchise and the story that I've seen in fan translations is really great. It is deeply, deeply disappointing to see the franchise decline in popularity while there is still exceptional quality content being produced and great music being released by the new groups. Other franchises with weaker stories and less interesting premises are eating Love Live's lunch as global fans turn their attention to franchises that are willing to engage global fans and make content accessible. I worry for the future of Love Live if they are not able/willing to capitalize on Bluebird, which has been really great to start and I believe has a ton of potential.
column66: I would love to see more Hasunosora content released to global audiences. I know there will be an animated movie, and that's a good start. But more people need to have access to those characters and that story. I also really hope Love Live makes an anime with Bluebird, because the premise of their setting is really promising and I feel like it could be extremely successful if produced and marketed correctly.

column65: im content with it. i think i reached the point of obsession when i was a younger teen so now that im older/more busy/been there done that im fine with the way things are. i would like a game, i dont agree with the ppl who say to just rerelease sif/sif2 again or reskin it. it wouldnt work. growth games like uma/gakumas are fine but i dont think its something i would particularly enjoy/would benefit love live in the long run since theres literally two giants there taking that space. i like how theyve been experimenting though i wish it happened a couple years earlier. the number one thing i would have to complain about is their inability to explore things outside pretty safe and easy story plots. seeing other franchises try darker things or more complex things is fun. does love live have to replicate that specifically? no. but i think the brick-wall-y-ness of their storytelling can be a bit of a bore. music is still solid. love the character designs. i hope to see love live continue to prosper well into my 80s
column66: um well i kind of already said but. an innovative game. simple rhythm game wont cut it anymore, the gameplay of all stars put off a lot of people, puchiguru 💔. hasu is fun but i never expected it to be available for me in eng. still enjoy it somewhat in jp just disconnected. yohane had fun ventures. i want to see the dev team innovate. i also want to see more interesting storylines, dramatics and stuff. i didnt mind the graduation because it showed that love live actually could stick to something and be different. i will still miss my girlies though.  i just want to see some sort of change. also a very specific rant: STOP DOING SO MANY TOURS/LIVES. theres a special and strong feeling that came from muse/early aqours lives because of their exclusivity. nowadays they finish a tour and get right back on the bus. theres not much novelty anymore.

column65: i am a bit concerned about the fans in the west/the lack of fans. there are def still fans of the series! my friends and i still love it. the thing is that you used to attend cons and see love live everywhere from merch to cosplays to people performing at the masquerades. now it's mainly things like project sekai or umamusume. there are still some merch and cosplayers but it doesn't seem as big as it once was and i'm talking the big cons like animenyc and anime expo. it does help when they bring the love live seiyuus to perform or do a panel at said cons and it brings the fans out but it def still isn't nearly as much as it once was when sifas was still around.
column66: i think having a good global app would def bring back the popularity and do more events. bring the love live movies to theaters in the us even if it's limited viewings. attend more cons and do more performance. allow fans to attend the concerts in japan as well. they have done it in the past where foreigners could enter a lottery to be able to get tickets to see love live in person. i think doing that more often and at a more reasonable price would help fans a lot. most things seem to be catered to japan which makes sense! it's a japanese franchise and that is where the money is at! understandable! it's still valid to be disappointed about the apps, concerts, and movies being region locked. which i don't get why people get upset at people who are disappointed. they say youre still able to download the app and play the game even if it's in japanese. sure sure you can. but you won't really be able to enjoy it as much if you can't understand what is actually happening and have to constantly look at a guide to do anything.

column65: It’s really bleak at the moment. Overseas accessibility is SO poor these days, and what there is feels pretty poorly thought out. I’d really like more content in general from Bluebird. I also personally am not a fan of the way that voting is handled for Bluebird in particular. I understand that new groups usually begin with a certain amount of fan input, but I’d much rather the characters have a cohesive point of view/theme themselves rather than having things like symbols, image color names, and emojis all decided by fans. This is probably an attempt to take advantage of the Twitter formatting, but since they aren’t doing much else other than the polls, it just doesn’t feel like they know what they want to do with these characters at all yet. And this isn’t something I’d usually mind, if not for the impression I’m getting that they’ll be implementing the graduation and real time aging system from Hasunosora (that I’m also not a fan of, since it really limits the time they have to tell a meaningful story). It’s frustrating, especially since these girls seem really cool!

column65: I think that Love Live is in a pretty well state. The content is coming in, there's still music, lives, stories from newer groups, and more. Although, like a lot of other global fans, I am really disappointed about the accessibility in their content overseas. No global games that really helped in pumping out more content (SIF & SIFAS) and they weren't really pushing the newer groups like Hasu or Bluebird as hard here compared to μ's, Aqours, or Nijigasaki (they kinda did with Liella but imo it was a lot less compared to the aforementioned three.) It's a bit harder to get into some of their content, especially given that I and a lot of people can be busy with life at times. I feel like Love Live is in a good state currently, but could be much MUCH better if they gave more options and made things more accessible for fans overseas.
column66: A GAME!!! I can't understate how a game would be so good right now. Just something like SIF or SIFAS, when they were out I never felt more connected with the community (hell, I made a daily theatre account thanks to SIFAS.) In the end, it doesn't have to be a game but just something to bring more fans together, something accessible that anyone and everyone can easily get into.

column65: its a bit awkward right now. no global projects is not a big issue to me, but i get why people are sad about it. personally, i fell in love with hasu at launch, and the story telling was incredible until 103 joined. the story started to feel very rushed and several character arcs were handled poorly (ie, megu being uncharacteristically rude by ignoring ruri's discomfort with a third member, ruri's character getting little development until i do me, tsutzuri and megu having extremely rushed 'closures' to their development, tsutzuri feeling very immature even by the end of her arc, etc). ikizuraibu feels like its trying to replicate hasu, but doesn't have a solid backbone to do so, as twitter would be a difficult medium to go back on for new fans. it feels like they got too overzealous at several points and are now trying to get back to the golden era of say, hasu storytelling. and they're trying to find their new big group with aqours' graduation.

column65: Franchise wise: great. Though Bluebird could do better as i feel the staff doesn't use twitter to its full potential (as in members replying to each other, or posting pics in regards to what they do or where they travel) / Fandom wise: atrocious. Most of the western fandom is ass: the english side is so blinded by nostalgia from the SIF era and actively WON'T engage with anything if it's not presented in a gacha game format. I feel like these SIF-only fans are really just gacha game fans, that's why most of them moved to Bandori, D4DJ, Project Sekai or recently Uma Musume, just because they need their "Gacha anime girl jpg app" fix. I mean, it's common sense that if they were as die hard fans of LL! as they said, they would find any of these franchises "lacking" what they loved about SIF (the groups, the music, etc.) and just wouldn't enjoy them as much, right? not to mention the SIF/SIFAS revisionism is WILD. People are acting like these games were perfect apps when SIF had the most dated UI of all rhythm games, the events were repeated over and over every year with minimal changes to the stories (like the setsubun or valentines stories) and the bond stories were so simple, or how they released really simple UR pairs at the end of the game. Not to mention SIFAS was super hated when it was released (despite having an actual engaging story, reminiscent of Hasu nowadays) just because it wasn't "SIF style" and rather a RPG/Idol Raising game. And also, it feels super insulting to the Muse seiyuus to expect for them to keep focusing their (already well established) careers into a character they did content 10-15 years ago?? / I also feel like the western just DOESN'T know a single thing about the idol/2.5 industry and culture and doesn't understand that LL! will ALWAYS be a franchise by Japanese, FOR Japanese. Not only that, but within that ignorance the USA-side fandom acts SUPER entitled? like the franchise OWES them an english version of everything they do, when ALL localized content that has existed is catered to THEM. like, up until the recent Spain show, the USA was the ONLY western country to have shows by not only a full group, but various subunits. Other than the recent console games, i don't think there is any content that has been localized for LATAM or any non-english speaking european country. This is why is so grating seeing these people complain about the content. / I also feel that people don't understand that if there's no oficial localized content, is because there aren't making enough sales here to actually make the staff start promoting here, simple as that. Why would the franchise create content for an area where their revenue is minimal compared to their domestic one? shops like CDJapan and Tokyo Otaku Mode have always been there for the western market to purchase content but if they never buy anything they will never look at here. / Finally, the spanish-speaking side of the western fandom, while not as stuck in SIF as their english counterpart, is equally or even more atrocious. Not only they are stuck in Facebook Groups like it's 2010, the communities are full of incel, mysogynistic pedophile weebs, that spend their time talking shit about any group, character or seiyuu that isn't their favorite. It's gotten so bad that i'm pretty sure any new fan that finds LL! from these communities adopts this trash-talking style of "being a fan" and replicates it (that is if they aren't disgusted of these people right away). It's a shame because the spanish side of the fandom has a lot of people translating the current generations content, but it gets eclipsed by those incel-ass environment. That's all.
column66: MORE experimentation in content. The franchise has been sooo good in this aspect and no one notice. They have been getting more established composers for their songs and getting into more niche and non-mainstream music genres since 2020, i hope they keep it up. Hasu being 100% real-time up to the point that the seiyuus only last a limited time too, while kind of disappointing if you actually like the character/seiyuu, it's a super transgressive concept and not only it goes so well in hand to the graduation concept of IRL groups like Morning Musume. or AKB48, it gives LL! and option to have a group of ever-revolving talents. Bluebird being online, while still slow at this point is a super interesting concept and it's the closest the franchise has been to the original 2010 muse/dengeki magazine-voting era. i feel like people don't really notice that. And of course, more cross-generation content. Not only they have a chance of a thing like AiScReam happening again, but also they give the seiyuus a chance to perform in limited groups flexible enough for their schedules without having to resort to their individual groups. / The only thing i feel needs a change in the franchise is for them to use a single style when drawing cross-generation content. Like i don't mind Niji changing styles all the time or Hasu/Bluebird looking more modern, but it looks super odd when multiple generations are together and everyone has their own style. Just stick to one style (whatever they chose) for these illustrations!

column65: While I (and many others) do wish we had a widely available game to play, I think a lot of fans just aren’t willing to admit there’s still lots of things going on! Ikuzulive is developing super fast, Hasu is still thriving and has lives next month, and there’s new art of most groups released all the time! Niji still has their monthly solos releasing, so on and so forth. As long as the fans continue to show up, even “inaccessible” content like the Hasu story will continue to be translated so we can all enjoy, even if it takes a little effort. I think they massively blundered what they had with the games - it was a series of bad decisions that led to a downward spiral, but this franchise isn’t just games. It’s so much more!

column65: I think Love Live! is in a decent spot right now. There is a healthy amount of content across all series, enough to keep fans of any group satisfied in my opinion. However, I do think the lack of internationally accessible content and subsequent language barrier are considerable obstacles in getting new overseas fans interested in the franchise. The death of School idol festival and lack of a proper new mobile game also greatly hurt, as while I had initially heard of Love Live! through online communities, it was playing SIF1 for myself that really got me invested the franchise.
column66: I would love to see another mobile game in the vein of School idol festival. I would also be very interested in the option of a paid, standalone rhythm game for smartphones that can be played offline. The rhythm game portion of SIF2 was highly underrated and one of its greatest strengths in my opinion, and I think it could be easily repurposed for this.

column65: It's in decline. The closing down of SIF/SIFAS/SIF2 eliminated one of the main ways I engaged with the franchise and its characters in a day to day basis and now I am more of a fan of Love Live as musical artists rather than as a multimedia project. It opened the door for other franchises such as Uma Musume to take the place Love Live had for me. Also, with Aqours having done their finale live and with Nijigasaki probably close to it, I worry about the franchise's future. I went to Japan for the Unit Koshien less than 2 years ago and I remember being quite optimistic about the future of Love Live after seeing Hasu perform, but after the graduations I lost all interest in them as one of the things I value the most about Love Live is stability. The graduated members not even appearing in the 15 anniversary promo art feels wrong. In a couple years, I might end up being just a fan of Liella as idols and don't care about anything else Love Live related if things continue to go this way.
column66: They need a new mobile game really badly, but not one that is a poorly made cash grab like SIF2, but something with actual heart. Also, I really hope we will continue seeing past groups (and members in Hasunosora's case) in lives, even if only some of them can attend at any given time.

column65: I love it, there's a lot of content, although not necessarily accessible. But apart from the accessibility I think there's so much to like and so much to look forward to. I think a lot of people don't like the direction they've recently taken which is fair, but tbvh I see this every few years. A franchise can't be stagnant, and naturally not everyone is gonna like everything they do. I've had this experience with other things I was a long time fan of and it really helped to just take the good times for what they were and move on when I found myself disliking every direction they took. Personally I'm a fan of whatever LL has been putting out thankfully and I'm excited for the future! Although definitely a bit more accessibility for all the wonderful things they have going on would be nice. I think the biggest connection to the global community were the games which no longer exist. So we def need something there.

column65: Love Live is regressing to a Japan only franchise. All new major content (Niji movies, Link Like) are only accessible in Japan. The international fan base is has a high concentration of apologists who are content with the way things are. Many of the content is available via fan translation, but bringing Love Live to a global audience shouldn't be a fan's job. On top of the fans doing all the work, it's steal from the creators. It also risking personal safety by doing things such as torrenting or sideloading. It really feels like the franchise really too the cheap route and slammed the door on anyone who isn't physically in Japan. We're just outsiders looking in.
column66: I want to see improvement in accessibility. It shouldn't be a fan's job to bring Love Live content to a global audience. It would be nice if Link Like released a global version even if the stories are updated later by a day or a few after updates in Japan to give translators time to work. This is just an example, I just want to see the effort. If there's something new, please share it with the world. Even if it takes time. I hope that the Blue Bird branch will accelerate and evolve in to more complex stuff like a game or an anime over time. Whatever it is, I hope the average person can enjoy it no matter where they live.
column67: Yes, but there could be more
